He was longing for peace, and there were some things that David was facing in his life, some circumstances that were adverse, and he was dealing with this great time of difficulty, and these circumstances drove him to his knees, it caused him to lift up his soul to the Lord, to seek the Lord, and in the time of difficulty, when he was needing peace, because of all of the chaos and the turmoil that was around him, he knew that he could go to Jehovah Shalom, the God who is our peace, and what a blessing to understand that, and at times, in the midst of our storms, in the midst of the storms of those that have gone before us, we need today, we need for our Savior, who is the Prince of Peace, to stand and say, peace, be still. As we continue looking through this Psalm, we note number two, and it's the lesson for this evening, that not only was David having a longing for peace, but we find throughout the Psalm here that there's a longing for direction. There's a longing for direction.
